questions.question: In a recipe for $45 \frac{3}{4}$ cups of flour, what is the value of the digit 4?
A building is $234.56$ meters tall. Rounded to the nearest hundred, what is its height?
The cost of $3 \frac{1}{2}$ acres of land is $12,000$. What is the cost per acre in standard form?
A chef needs $15 \frac{3}{4}$ kilograms of sugar. What is this in expanded form?
The length of a room is $12.34$ meters. Rounded to the nearest tenth, what is its length?
A recipe calls for $0.45$ liters of oil. What is this in standard form?
A water tank can hold $456.78$ liters. What is the value of the digit 6?
The cost of a meal is $23.49$. Rounded to the nearest dollar, what is the cost?
The area of a rectangular garden is $25.6$ square meters. If its length is $5.2$ meters, what is its width in standard form? Show your calculations.
A bakery sells $234.56$ kilograms of bread per day. If they sell it in packs of $1.25$ kilograms, how many packs do they sell per day? Show your calculations.

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Encourage students to use the place value chart to identify digit values.
Tip 2: Remind students that rounding numbers depends on the digit to the right of the place value.
Tip 3: Emphasize the importance of using the correct form (standard or expanded) when writing numbers.
